testcase.name: Given the salt is "1", the feature is "f1" and the id is "alice"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "de5ce0fbc583fd8"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 9
Given the salt is "1", the feature is "f1" and the id is "bob"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "8ecddc9f392dc35"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 14
Given the salt is "2", the feature is "f1" and the id is "alice"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "e31eff9e88214f2"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 71
Given the salt is "2", the feature is "f1" and the id is "bob"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "591e96e46fc1dad"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 58
Given the salt is "3", the feature is "f1" and the id is "alice"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "05ad8a286f0b0bb"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 36
Given the salt is "3", the feature is "f1" and the id is "bob"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "9bc2af62801255d"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 2
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "not-my-test-value"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "98"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "98"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "98"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "10"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "-test-v"
When the operator test "contains" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "nope"
When the operator test "contains" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
When the operator test "contains" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value-two"
When the operator test "contains" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test"
When the operator test "startsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "-test-v"
When the operator test "startsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
When the operator test "startsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value-two"
When the operator test "startsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "test-value"
When the operator test "endsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "-test-v"
When the operator test "endsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
When the operator test "endsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "long-my-test-value"
When the operator test "endsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my.test.+"
When the operator test "matches" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "wont-match"
When the operator test "matches" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "^[A-z\-]+$"
When the operator test "matches" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "^[A-z]+$"
When the operator test "matches" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "doesn't matter"
When the operator test "fakeOperator" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "two"
And the attribute is an array of values "one, two, three"
When the operator test "in" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "four"
And the attribute is an array of values "one, two, three"
When the operator test "in" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "four"
And the attribute is an array of values "one, two, three"
When the operator test "notIn" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "two"
And the attribute is an array of values "one, two, three"
When the operator test "notIn" is run
Then the output should equal "false"

Then the output should equal "true"

Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-04-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "before" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-02-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "before" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "before" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-04-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "after" is run
Then the output should equal "false"

And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-02-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "after" is run
Then the output should equal "true"

And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "after" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2026-07-21T02:00:00-05:00"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2026-07-21T06:00:00Z"
When the operator test "after" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2026-07-21T23:00:00+10:00"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2026-07-21T14:00:00Z"
When the operator test "before" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "not-a-date"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2026-07-21T06:00:00Z"
When the operator test "after" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "not-a-date"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2026-07-21T06:00:00Z"
When the operator test "before" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given an events client
When 3 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
Then the pending summary should contain 1 entries
And the pending summary for feature "f1" variant "on" should have 3 impressions
Given an events client
When 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
And 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "off" user "u2"
Then the pending summary should contain 2 entries
And the pending summary for feature "f1" variant "on" should have 1 impressions
And the pending summary for feature "f1" variant "off" should have 1 impressions
Given an events client with a summary capacity of 2
When 3 evaluate events are queued
Then the pending summary should contain 2 entries
Given an events client with a summary capacity of 1
When 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
And 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f2" variant "on" user "u2"
And 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
Then the pending summary should contain 1 entries
And the pending summary for feature "f1" variant "on" should have 2 impressions
Given an events client
When 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
And 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f2" variant "on" user "u1"
Then the pending summary entry for feature "f1" variant "on" should include user "u1"
And the pending summary entry for feature "f2" variant "on" should include no users
Given a local events endpoint that responds with status 200
And an events client pointed at the local endpoint
When 2 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
And 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u2"
And the event queue is flushed
Then the local endpoint should have received a batch of 2 events
And the batch should total 3 impressions for feature "f1" variant "on"
And the batch should include users "u1" and "u2"
And the batch events should not include an expectedVariant
Given a local events endpoint that responds with status 200
And an events client pointed at the local endpoint
When 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
And the event queue is flushed
And 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
Then the pending summary entry for feature "f1" variant "on" should include user "u1"
Given a local events endpoint that responds with status 401
And an events client pointed at the local endpoint
When 2 evaluate events are queued
And the event queue is flushed
Then the events client should become disabled
And the pending summary should contain 0 entries
And queueing another evaluate event should leave the summary empty
Given a local events endpoint that responds with status 403
And an events client pointed at the local endpoint
When 2 evaluate events are queued
And the event queue is flushed
Then the events client should become disabled
Given a local events endpoint that responds with status 429 and Retry-After 60
And an events client pointed at the local endpoint
When 2 evaluate events are queued
And the event queue is flushed
Then the events client should be backing off
And the pending summary should contain 2 entries
When the event queue is flushed
Then the local endpoint should have received 1 request
Given a local events endpoint that responds with status 429 and Retry-After 60
And an events client pointed at the local endpoint
When 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
And the event queue is flushed
Then the events client should be backing off
When 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
Then the pending summary for feature "f1" variant "on" should have 2 impressions
Given a local events endpoint that responds with status 429
And an events client pointed at the local endpoint
When 1 evaluate events are queued
And the event queue is flushed
Then the events client should be backing off
Given a Featureflow client with the stored features
When evaluateAll is called for user "user-1"
Then the evaluated features should be
And no evaluate events should have been recorded
Given a Featureflow client with the stored features
When evaluate "f1" is called for user "user-1" and isOn is checked
Then 1 evaluate event should have been recorded
Given the feature "my-feature" with an offVariantKey "off", a default key of "on" is disabled
When the feature is evaluated with a user "user"
Then the evaluated variant should be "off"
Given the feature "my-feature" with an offVariantKey "off", a default key of "on" is enabled
When the feature is evaluated with a user "user"
Then the evaluated variant should be "on"
Given an events client
When goal "signup" is tracked for user "u1"
Then the pending goals should contain 1 events
And the pending goal "signup" should have user "u1"
Given an events client
When goal "checkout-value" is tracked for user "u1" with value 129.9
Then the pending goal "checkout-value" should have value 129.9
Given an events client
When goal "purchase" is tracked for user "u1" with details
Then the pending goal "purchase" should have value 5
And the pending goal "purchase" should have data
Given a local events endpoint that responds with status 200
And an events client pointed at the local endpoint
When 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
And goal "signup" is tracked for user "u1"
And the event queue is flushed
Then the local endpoint should have received a batch of 2 events
And the batch should include a goal event "signup" with type "goal" and no featureKey
Given a local events endpoint that responds with status 200
And an events client pointed at the local endpoint
When goal "signup" is tracked for user "u1"
And the event queue is flushed
Then the local endpoint should have received a batch of 1 events
Given an events client
When the server config is applied
And goal "signup" is tracked for user "u1"
Then the pending goals should contain 0 events
Given a local events endpoint that responds with status 429 and Retry-After 60
And an events client pointed at the local endpoint
When goal "signup" is tracked for user "u1"
And the event queue is flushed
Then the events client should be backing off
And the pending goals should contain 1 events
Given the feature "my-feature" evaluates to variant "on" with variants
When jsonValue is called
Then the json value should equal
Given the feature "my-feature" evaluates to variant "off" with variants
When jsonValue is called
Then the json value should be undefined
Given the feature "my-feature" evaluates to variant "on" with no variants
When jsonValue is called
Then the json value should be undefined
Given the feature "my-feature" evaluates to variant "on" with variants
When value is called
Then the result should be "on"
Given the feature "my-feature" evaluates to variant "on" with variants
When jsonValue is called
Then an evaluate event should be queued with featureKey "my-feature" and evaluatedVariant "on"
And the queued event should only have keys "featureKey, evaluatedVariant, user"
Given the rule is a default rule
When the rule is matched against the user
Then the result from the match should be true
Given the user attributes are
And the rule's audience conditions are
When the rule is matched against the user
Then the result from the match should be true
Given the user attributes are
And the rule's audience conditions are
When the rule is matched against the user
Then the result from the match should be false

And the rule's audience conditions are
When the rule is matched against the user
Then the result from the match should be true

When the rule is matched against the user
Then the result from the match should be true

And the rule's audience conditions are
When the rule is matched against the user
Then the result from the match should be false

And the rule's audience conditions are
When the rule is matched against the user
Then the result from the match should be true

And the rule's audience conditions are
When the rule is matched against the user
Then the result from the match should be true
Given the user attributes are
And the rule's audience conditions are
When the rule is matched against the user
Then the result from the match should be false
Given the variant value of 50
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "off"
Given the variant value of 50
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "on"
Given the variant value of 11
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "on"
Given the variant value of 9
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "off"
Given the variant value of 1
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "on"
Given the variant value of 30
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "on"
Given the variant value of 50
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "alpha"
Given the variant value of 61
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "beta"
Given the variant value of 100
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "betav2"
Given an events client
When 2 evaluate events are queued
And the server config is applied
Then the pending summary should contain 0 entries
When 1 evaluate events are queued
Then the pending summary should contain 0 entries
Given an events client
When the server config is applied
And 1 evaluate events are queued
And the server config is applied
And 1 evaluate events are queued
Then the pending summary should contain 1 entries
Given an events client
When the server config is applied
And 2 evaluate events are queued
Then the pending summary should contain 0 entries
Given an events client
When the server config is applied
And 2 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
Then the pending summary should contain 2 entries
And every pending entry should have 1 impression and user "u1"
Given an events client
When the server config is applied
Then the events client send interval should be 30 seconds
Given an events client
When the server config is applied
Then the events client send interval should be 60 seconds
And the events client should not be suspended
When 1 evaluate events are queued
Then the pending summary should contain 1 entries
Given a disabled events client
When the server config is applied
And 1 evaluate events are queued
Then the pending summary should contain 0 entries
Given a local events endpoint that responds with status 200 and config body
And an events client pointed at the local endpoint
When 1 evaluate events are queued
And the event queue is flushed
Then the events client should become suspended
And the events client send interval should be 120 seconds
Given a local features endpoint with config header
And a Featureflow client pointed at the local features endpoint
Then the events client should become suspended
And the events client send interval should be 300 seconds
Given a local features endpoint with config header
And a polling Featureflow client pointed at the local features endpoint
Then the polling interval should become 60 seconds
Given a local features endpoint with config header
And a Featureflow client pointed at the local features endpoint
Then the polling interval should remain 0
Given a local features endpoint whose features change on every request
And a polling Featureflow client pointed at the local features endpoint
When the features are refreshed
Then the client should have emitted 1 updated event
Given an events client
When 1 evaluate events are queued for tracked feature "exp1" variant "on" user "u1"
And 1 evaluate events are queued for tracked feature "exp1" variant "on" user "u2"
And 1 evaluate events are queued for tracked feature "exp1" variant "on" user "u1"
Then the pending summary for feature "exp1" variant "on" should have 3 impressions
And the pending summary entry for feature "exp1" variant "on" should include user "u1"
And the pending summary entry for feature "exp1" variant "on" should include user "u2"
Given an events client
When 1 evaluate events are queued for feature "f1" variant "on" user "u1"
And 1 evaluate events are queued for tracked feature "exp1" variant "on" user "u1"
Then the pending summary entry for feature "exp1" variant "on" should include user "u1"
Given an events client
When 2 evaluate events are queued for tracked feature "exp1" variant "on" user "u1"
Then the pending summary for feature "exp1" variant "on" should have 2 impressions
And the pending summary entry for feature "exp1" variant "on" should include only user "u1"
Given a Featureflow client with the stored features
When evaluate "exp1" is called for user "user-1" and isOn is checked
And evaluate "f1" is called for user "user-1" and isOn is checked
Then the recorded evaluate event for "exp1" should have trackEvents true
And the recorded evaluate event for "f1" should have trackEvents false
Given there is access to the User Builder module
When the builder is initialised with the id "user"
And the user is built using the builder
Then the result user should have an id "user"
And the result user should have no attributes
Given there is access to the User Builder module
When the builder is initialised with the id "user"
And the builder is given the following attributes
And the user is built using the builder
Then the result user should have an id "user"
And the result user should have a attribute with key "age" and value "21"
And the result user should have a attribute with key "type" and value "beta"
Given there is access to the User Builder module
When the builder is initialised with the id "user"
And the builder is given the following attributes
And the user is built using the builder
Then the result user should have a attribute with key "featureflow.user.id" and value "user"
And the result user should have a attribute with key "featureflow.date" and current datetime in iso8601
Given there is access to the User Builder module
When the builder is initialised with the id ""
Then the builder should throw an error
